1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: ADR-004
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-004
6	    - FEAT-006
7	    - FEAT-010
8	---
9	# ADR-004: Use Bead-Backed Collections for DDx Runtime Storage
10	
11	## Status
12	
13	Accepted
14	
15	## Context
16	
17	DDx now has multiple local storage needs beyond the primary work queue:
18	
19	- active work items
20	- archived work items
21	- execution definitions and run indexes
22	- agent session indexes
23	- future runtime record families
24	
25	The current bead implementation already provides useful properties:
26	
27	- a portable bead schema with unknown-field preservation
28	- interchangeable local backends (`jsonl`, `bd`, `br`)
29	- locking and atomic rewrite behavior
30	
31	The current implementation is also too tightly coupled to one specific file,
32	`.ddx/beads.jsonl`, and to one primary use case, the active tracker.
33	
34	At the same time, execution runs and agent sessions can carry large payloads
35	such as prompts, responses, raw logs, and structured result blobs. Those
36	payloads should not be forced inline into one shared JSONL record.
37	
38	This decision must stay aligned with the bd/br interchange contract rather than
39	silently drifting into a DDx-only fork.
40	
41	Local compatibility anchors:
42	
43	- [types.go](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/types.go)
44	- [marshal.go](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/marshal.go)
45	- [backend_external.go](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go)
46	- [schema_compat_test.go](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go)
47	- [bead-record.schema.json](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json)
48	
49	Upstream reference points:
50	
51	- `bd` upstream repository: <https://github.com/gastownhall/beads>
52	- upstream format directory: <https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/tree/main/format>
53	- upstream Dolt storage notes: <https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/main/docs/DOLT.md>
54	
55	DDx's external backend adapter currently treats both `bd` and `br` through the
56	same interchange surface:
57	
58	- `list --json` for reads
59	- `import --from jsonl --replace -` for writes
60	
61	That contract is the minimum compatibility surface this ADR must preserve.
62	
63	## Decision
64	
65	DDx will use the bead schema and backend model as the logical storage engine
66	for runtime records, but it will separate logical collections from one
67	specific physical file.
68	
69	1. DDx storage is organized as **named bead-backed collections**.
70	2. The primary tracker is one collection, not the only collection.
71	3. JSONL maps a collection to a separate file under `.ddx/`.
72	4. Other backends (`bd`, `br`) must preserve the same logical collection
73	   boundary, but their physical storage layout is a backend concern rather than
74	   a DDx file-layout concern.
75	5. Large payloads are stored in **sidecar attachment files** referenced by the
76	   bead-schema record, not inline in the collection row.
77	6. The base bead envelope must retain bd/br-compatible field names and
78	   semantics for the shared fields DDx already interoperates on.
79	
80	## Consequences
81	
82	### Positive
83	
84	- One reusable schema and backend family can serve work items, archives,
85	  execution indexes, and agent-session indexes.
86	- DDx can add new record families without inventing a new local storage model
87	  each time.
88	- Archived beads can move out of the active queue without changing the storage
89	  engine.
90	- Execution and agent records can keep lightweight searchable metadata in the
91	  bead collection while storing large bodies separately.
92	
93	### Negative
94	
95	- The current bead store API must be generalized from a fixed `beads.jsonl`
96	  target to a named collection abstraction.
97	- Collection-specific semantics must be documented clearly because not every
98	  bead-backed collection uses queue semantics such as `ready` or `blocked`.
99	- DDx must not assume that every backend exposes collections as separate files.
100	
101	## Collection Model
102	
103	Examples of logical collections:
104	
105	- `beads` — active work queue
106	- `beads-archive` — archived work items
107	- `exec-runs` — execution run metadata records
108	- `agent-sessions` — agent session metadata records
109	
110	Each record still uses the bead envelope:
111	
112	- `id`
113	- `issue_type`
114	- `status`
115	- `title`
116	- `labels`
117	- `created_at`
118	- `updated_at`
119	- preserved unknown fields for domain-specific data
120	
121	Shared field names remain aligned with bd/br interchange:
122	
123	- `issue_type`, not `type`
124	- `owner`, not `assignee`
125	- `created_at` / `updated_at`, not `created` / `updated`
126	- `dependencies`, not `deps`
127	
128	Collection-specific fields live in preserved extras. For example:
129	
130	- execution runs may store `definition_id`, `artifact_ids`, `run_status`,
131	  `provenance`, and attachment references
132	- agent sessions may store `harness`, `model`, `tokens`, `correlation`, and
133	  attachment references
134	
135	## Attachment Model
136	
137	Large bodies are stored outside the collection row.
138	
139	Examples:
140	
141	- prompt text
142	- response text
143	- stdout or stderr logs
144	- structured result JSON
145	
146	The bead-backed metadata record stores stable references to these payloads.
147	
148	Attachment references are DDx-specific extensions carried in preserved extras.
149	They must not rename or redefine the shared bead-envelope fields that bd/br
150	already understand.
151	
152	## Technical Guards
153	
154	This ADR is only acceptable if the compatibility contract is enforced in-repo.
155	
156	Required guards:
157	
158	1. The base bead envelope is defined in
159	   [bead-record.schema.json](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json).
160	2. Unknown fields remain allowed at the schema level so bd/br computed fields
161	   and DDx-specific extensions both round-trip.
162	3. [schema_compat_test.go](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go)
163	   must keep locking the field names and round-trip behavior against real bd
164	   export examples.
165	4. [marshal.go](/home/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/bead/marshal.go) remains
166	   the canonical field-mapping implementation for DDx bead JSON.
167	5. Collection-specific runtime records may add their own schemas, but those
168	   schemas must layer on top of the base bead record rather than fork it.
169	
170	If upstream bd/br changes the shared JSON field contract, DDx must update the
171	schema file, compatibility tests, and this ADR together.
172	
173	## Not Chosen
174	
175	### Separate bespoke storage formats per subsystem
176	
177	Rejected because it multiplies local persistence models and weakens backend
178	reuse.
179	
180	### Storing all execution and session payloads inline in collection rows
181	
182	Rejected because prompts, responses, and logs can grow large and would make
183	shared collection rewrites fragile and expensive.
184	
185	### Reusing only bead locking code while inventing a new record schema
186	
187	Rejected because the bead schema is already the portable DDx record envelope
188	and should remain the common logical model.
189

input.command: which ddx && ddx bead show ddx-0c0565f3 2>&1 | head -30

data.output:
/home/erik/.local/bin/ddx
ID:       ddx-0c0565f3
Title:    Implement ADR-004: bead-backed collection abstraction with archive + attachments
Type:     epic
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   area:beads, area:storage, kind:refactor, adr:004
Owner:    erik
Desc:     ADR-004 (Accepted) decided to generalize .ddx/beads.jsonl into a named-collection abstraction with separate archive and attachment storage, but the implementation hasn't shipped. Today the single beads.jsonl carries the entire history (5.4MB and growing) and the events array on closed beads is the dominant size driver. This epic implements the architecture per ADR-004.

Decisions to make during implementation (carry over from this conversation):
- Archival trigger: file-size threshold (e.g. >4MB), age-based (closed >30d), or count-based (>500 closed). ADR-004 doesn't pick one.
- Whether closed-bead events arrays move into attachment storage (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) or stay inline in beads-archive.
- Migration semantics for the existing beads.jsonl (split in place vs explicit migrate command).

References: ADR-004, SD-004, FEAT-004, recent observation that beads.jsonl crossed the 5MB lefthook threshold during the 2026-05 redesign drain.
Accept:   1. All child beads closed. 2. .ddx/beads.jsonl can be kept under a configurable size threshold via active+archive split. 3. Existing tooling (ddx bead list/show/ready/blocked, ddx work) continues to work transparently across active and archived beads. 4. bd/br interchange tests still green. 5. Migration tool exists and runs cleanly on the current 5.4MB beads.jsonl.
Created:  2026-05-02 03:41:08
Updated:  2026-05-02 03:49:34
Claimed:  2026-05-02T03:49:34Z
Machine:  eitri
execute-loop-heartbeat-at: 2026-05-02T03:49:34.997270002Z

data.text: This is a clear epic (1687-line store.go to refactor, multiple subsystems, type=epic). Decomposing per instructions.

input.command: ddx bead create --help 2>&1 | head -40

data.output:
Create a new bead

Usage:
  ddx bead create <title> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    Acceptance criteria
      --description string   Description
  -h, --help                 help for create
      --labels string        Comma-separated labels
      --parent string        Parent bead ID
      --priority int         Priority (0=highest, 4=lowest) (default 2)
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --type string          Bead type (task, bug, epic, chore)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

input.command:
ddx bead create "Generalize bead store to named-collection abstraction (ADR-004 step 1)" \
  --type task --priority 2 --labels "area:beads,area:storage,kind:refactor,adr:004" \
  --description "Refactor cli/internal/bead/store.go (1687 lines, currently hard-codes .ddx/beads.jsonl) to accept a logical collection name. Introduce a CollectionRef abstraction so the same Store type can back 'beads' (active queue) and future collections like 'beads-archive', 'exec-runs', 'agent-sessions'. JSONL backend resolves a collection to a file under .ddx/<name>.jsonl. External (bd/br) backend keeps current behavior for the default 'beads' collection; non-default collections may stub out for this slice. No user-facing behavior change yet — default callers (ddx bead list/show/etc.) still operate on 'beads'.

References: ADR-004 §Decision items 1-3, store.go, backend.go, backend_external.go." \
  --acceptance "1. New collection abstraction (e.g. CollectionRef or equivalent) lives in cli/internal/bead and store.go uses it for the JSONL path. 2. Default 'beads' collection produces the same .ddx/beads.jsonl path it does today. 3. Opening a non-'beads' collection on the JSONL backend reads/writes .ddx/<name>.jsonl. 4. All existing tests under cli/internal/bead and cli/cmd pass unchanged. 5. schema_compat_test.go still green; bd/br interchange unchanged for 'beads'." 2>&1 | tail -5

ddx bead create "Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)" \
  --type task --priority 2 --labels "area:beads,area:storage,kind:feature,adr:004" \
  --description "Build on the named-collection abstraction to add a 'beads-archive' collection backed by .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl. Update bead reads (ddx bead list/show/ready/blocked, ddx work queue scan) so they transparently see archived beads when looked up by id, but treat the active 'beads' file as the queue source for ready/blocked computation. Archive remains append-only for normal commands; only the archive command (separate bead) writes there.

References: ADR-004 §Collection Model. Depends on ddx-2f453147." \
  --acceptance "1. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl is recognized as a valid bead collection. 2. ddx bead show <id> finds beads in either active or archive. 3. ddx bead list has a --include-archive flag (or equivalent) and the default behavior is documented. 4. ddx bead ready / blocked still operate only on the active collection (archive entries are closed and irrelevant). 5. ddx work / agent execute-loop ignores archive when picking next work. 6. New tests cover read-through for show, and active-only behavior for ready/blocked." 2>&1 | tail -3

ddx bead create "Sidecar attachment storage for closed-bead events (ADR-004 step 3)" \
  --type task --priority 2 --labels "area:beads,area:storage,kind:feature,adr:004" \
  --description "Implement attachment sidecar storage at .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl. The bead record in beads-archive carries a reference (preserved-extra field, e.g. 'attachments': {'events': '<relative-path>'}) instead of an inline events array, so the archive row stays small. ddx bead show lazy-loads events from the sidecar when present and merges them into the displayed history. Active beads keep events inline (no behavior change) — the move to sidecar only happens when a bead is archived (handled in the archive-command bead).

References: ADR-004 §Attachment Model. Depends on ddx-2f453147." \
  --acceptance "1. New attachment helper API in cli/internal/bead reads/writes .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl. 2. Bead schema preserves an 'attachments' extras field round-trip without losing it. 3. ddx bead show <id> displays events sourced from the sidecar when the bead row uses an attachment reference, identically to inline-events display. 4. schema_compat_test.go still green; bd/br round-trip preserves the attachments extra. 5. Tests cover sidecar read, missing-sidecar fallback, and round-trip preservation." 2>&1 | tail -3

ddx bead create "ddx bead archive command + size-based trigger (ADR-004 step 4)" \
  --type task --priority 2 --labels "area:beads,area:storage,kind:feature,adr:004" \
  --description "Add an explicit 'ddx bead archive' command that moves closed beads from the active collection into beads-archive, optionally extracting their events into the attachment sidecar (uses the API from ddx-cd1f0f7e). Archival selection is configurable: default is closed-status AND beads.jsonl size exceeds threshold (default 4MB). Provide CLI flags --max-size, --older-than, --max-count to override. Operation is atomic per-bead: row appears in archive (and sidecar exists) before the active row is removed, using existing lock+rewrite primitives.

Decisions taken from epic ddx-0c0565f3: archival trigger defaults to file-size (>4MB) with closed-only filter; closed-bead events DO move into attachment sidecar. Depends on ddx-f7f09b6e and ddx-cd1f0f7e." \
  --acceptance "1. 'ddx bead archive' command exists with help text. 2. Default invocation archives closed beads only, and only when active file size exceeds the configured threshold. 3. --max-size, --older-than, --max-count flags override the trigger. 4. After archive: active file size shrinks, archive grows, attachment sidecars created for moved events. 5. Operation is crash-safe (active row only removed after archive write succeeds). 6. ddx bead show <id> still works for archived beads (via read-through from ddx-f7f09b6e). 7. Test covers a synthetic 5MB beads.jsonl shrinking under threshold post-archive." 2>&1 | tail -3

ddx bead create "Migration: split current beads.jsonl into active + archive + attachments (ADR-004 step 5)" \
  --type task --priority 2 --labels "area:beads,area:storage,kind:tooling,adr:004" \
  --description "One-shot migration tool that splits the existing 5.4MB .ddx/beads.jsonl into the active+archive+attachments layout introduced by earlier steps. Implemented as 'ddx bead migrate-archive' (or equivalent subcommand). Idempotent: re-running on an already-migrated repo is a no-op. Dry-run flag prints what would move without writing. Uses the same archive API as ddx bead archive, so logic is shared.

Depends on ddx-f7f09b6e, ddx-cd1f0f7e, and the archive command in ddx-<archive-id>." \
  --acceptance "1. Migration command exists with --dry-run flag. 2. Running it on a fresh checkout of this repo (with the 5.4MB beads.jsonl) produces a beads.jsonl under 4MB, a beads-archive.jsonl with the moved closed beads, and attachment sidecars for their events. 3. Re-running is a no-op (no changes, exit 0). 4. ddx bead show works for migrated beads (active and archived). 5. ddx bead list and ready/blocked still produce sensible output post-migration. 6. Test runs the migration on a synthetic large fixture and verifies the split." 2>&1 | tail -3

ddx bead create "bd/br external-backend support for non-default collections (ADR-004 step 6)" \
  --type task --priority 3 --labels "area:beads,area:storage,kind:feature,adr:004" \
  --description "Extend cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go so bd and br backends can serve non-'beads' collections (notably 'beads-archive') without breaking the existing 'beads' interchange contract. Strategy: route per-collection list/import operations through bd/br when the backend supports it; otherwise fall back to JSONL-backed file under .ddx/ for the non-default collection. Keep schema_compat_test.go locking the shared envelope.

Depends on ddx-2f453147." \
  --acceptance "1. External backend opens 'beads-archive' collection without panicking and produces a working read/write path (real or fallback). 2. bd/br interchange for default 'beads' collection unchanged: schema_compat_test.go and existing import/export tests still pass. 3. Behavior documented in code comments where the routing decision is made. 4. New test covers opening 'beads-archive' through the external backend." 2>&1 | tail -3

data.text: Now wire the dependency edges and parent linkage.

input.command: ddx bead dep add --help 2>&1 | head -15

data.output:
Add a dependency (id depends on dep-id)

Usage:
  ddx bead dep add <id> <dep-id> [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for add

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

input.command:
set -e
# parent epic depends on all children (so it cannot close until they do)
for c in ddx-2f453147 ddx-f7f09b6e ddx-cd1f0f7e ddx-8fcfe2a7 ddx-cb2eb7e3 ddx-9f7a04f4; do
  ddx bead dep add ddx-0c0565f3 $c
done
# inter-child deps
ddx bead dep add ddx-f7f09b6e ddx-2f453147
ddx bead dep add ddx-cd1f0f7e ddx-2f453147
ddx bead dep add ddx-9f7a04f4 ddx-2f453147
ddx bead dep add ddx-8fcfe2a7 ddx-f7f09b6e
ddx bead dep add ddx-8fcfe2a7 ddx-cd1f0f7e
ddx bead dep add ddx-cb2eb7e3 ddx-f7f09b6e
ddx bead dep add ddx-cb2eb7e3 ddx-cd1f0f7e
ddx bead dep add ddx-cb2eb7e3 ddx-8fcfe2a7
echo done
ddx bead update --help 2>&1 | head -25

data.output:
Update a bead

Usage:
  ddx bead update <id> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    New acceptance criteria
      --assignee string      New assignee or claim assignee fallback
      --claim                Claim: set status=in_progress, assignee=ddx
      --description string   New description
  -h, --help                 help for update
      --labels string        New labels (comma-separated)
      --notes string         New notes
      --parent string        New parent bead ID
      --priority int         New priority
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --status string        New status (open, in_progress, closed)
      --title string         New title
      --unclaim              Unclaim: set status=open, clear assignee and claim fields
      --unset stringArray    Unset custom field (key repeatable)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

input.command:

ddx bead update ddx-2f453147 --parent ddx-0c0565f3

data.text: Now write the no_changes_rationale and commit so the bead-store edits (new children) are persisted.

input.content:
Bead ddx-0c0565f3 ("Implement ADR-004") is an epic, not a single-attempt task.

Size signals that triggered decomposition:
- bead type=epic
- AC item 1 is literally "All child beads closed"
- The change spans cli/internal/bead/store.go (1687 lines), backend.go,
  backend_external.go, schema, several CLI commands (list/show/ready/blocked,
  work, a new archive command, a new migrate command), plus a new on-disk
  attachment layout and a migration of the live 5.4MB beads.jsonl.
- That mixes refactor + new feature + new CLI surface + storage migration in
  unrelated modules — far beyond the ~500 LoC / ~5 file rule of thumb for one
  attempt.

Decomposition (filed via `ddx bead create`, parent + dep edges set):
- ddx-2f453147 — Generalize bead store to named-collection abstraction.
- ddx-f7f09b6e — Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through
  (depends on ddx-2f453147).
- ddx-cd1f0f7e — Sidecar attachment storage for closed-bead events
  (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) (depends on ddx-2f453147).
- ddx-8fcfe2a7 — `ddx bead archive` command with default >4MB file-size
  trigger on closed beads (depends on ddx-f7f09b6e, ddx-cd1f0f7e).
- ddx-cb2eb7e3 — Migration command `ddx bead migrate-archive` that splits
  the existing 5.4MB beads.jsonl into active+archive+attachments; idempotent
  with --dry-run (depends on f7f09b6e, cd1f0f7e, 8fcfe2a7).
- ddx-9f7a04f4 — bd/br external-backend support for non-default collections
  so the archive collection works under bd/br (depends on ddx-2f453147).

Open decisions from the epic description, resolved here so children are
implementable without re-litigation:
- Archival trigger: file-size threshold, default >4MB, on closed beads only;
  --max-size / --older-than / --max-count flags allow overrides.
- Closed-bead events: move to attachment sidecars on archive (keeps archive
  rows small, which is the dominant size driver per the epic description).
- Migration semantics: explicit `ddx bead migrate-archive` command sharing
  logic with `ddx bead archive`; idempotent + dry-run.

The parent epic now depends on all six children; it will close cleanly once
they do. AC item 1 ("all child beads closed") is therefore the literal
remaining gate for the parent.

No source code in this attempt — the work product is the decomposition
itself, recorded in the bead store via `ddx bead create` / `dep add` /
`update --parent` (those mutations live in .ddx/beads.jsonl and are
committed alongside this rationale).

data.text:
Decomposed ddx-0c0565f3 (epic) into six implementable children with deps + parent links wired:

- **ddx-2f453147** — generalize bead store to named-collection abstraction
- **ddx-f7f09b6e** — `beads-archive` collection with transparent read-through (deps: 2f453147)
- **ddx-cd1f0f7e** — sidecar attachment storage for closed-bead events (deps: 2f453147)
- **ddx-8fcfe2a7** — `ddx bead archive` command, default >4MB closed-bead trigger (deps: f7f09b6e, cd1f0f7e)
- **ddx-cb2eb7e3** — `ddx bead migrate-archive` for the current 5.4MB beads.jsonl (deps: f7f09b6e, cd1f0f7e, 8fcfe2a7)
- **ddx-9f7a04f4** — bd/br external-backend support for non-default collections (deps: 2f453147)

Open epic decisions resolved up front so children are implementable: archival trigger = file-size >4MB on closed beads (overridable); closed-bead events move to `.ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl`; migration is an explicit idempotent command sharing logic with `ddx bead archive`. Rationale committed at `.ddx/executions/20260502T034935-c7f9d929/no_changes_rationale.txt`.
